file.content:
# Trivivo

## Repository
- **Repo**: `steadyfall/wwbm-webapp`
- **Main branch**: `main`

## Git & Commits

**Branch naming**: `<username>/descriptive-name` (e.g. `steadyfall/tri-60-test-usernamevalidator-...`)

Max character limit for branch name: 60 characters total

**Commit messages**: Conventional Commits format
```
<type>: short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `test`, `build`, `chore`, `style`, `refactor`

## Pull Requests

- **Always create PRs as drafts** (`--draft`) targeting `main`
- **Do not include ticket IDs in PR titles** (no `TRI-xx` or issue numbers)
- **Follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`**:
  ```
  Fixes <linear-ticket-or-issue>

  ### Description
  <!-- problem being fixed -->

  ### Tests
  <!-- tests written/done -->
  ```
- Keep PR body ≤ 500 words
- Include screenshots/pictures for any frontend/visual changes

## Testing

**Framework**: `pytest` with `pytest-django`

**Config**: `pyproject.toml`
```
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=kbc.settings
DJANGO_CONFIGURATION=Dev
```

**Before running tests, source the env file**:
```bash
source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file> -v --tb=short
```

**Rules**:
- All tests must pass — no skips, no failures
- Use `@pytest.mark.django_db` for any test that touches the database
- Integration tests use `django.test.Client`
- Unit tests for validators/pure logic require no DB

**Test file location**: `tests/unit/`

## General Conventions

- **Read source code before writing tests** — never assume redirect URLs, message levels, or behavior; read the actual view/function first
- Use git worktrees when working on parallel feature branches to avoid interfering with `main`
- Run `make setup` when in freshly initialized git worktree
- Source `.env` in every worktree (it is not copied automatically)

file.content:
[project]
name = "trivivo"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Just a trivia game."
requires-python = ">=3.11"
dependencies = [
    "Django>=5.1.2",
    "djangorestframework>=3.14.0",
    "django-configurations>=2.5.1",
    "django-crispy-forms>=2.0",
    "crispy-bootstrap5>=0.7",
    "django-tailwind>=3.8.0",
    "django-browser-reload>=1.12.0",
    "dj-database-url>=2.2.0",
    "dj-email-url>=1.0.6",
    "dj-search-url>=0.1",
    "django-cache-url>=3.4.5",
    "aiohttp>=3.11.11",
    "requests>=2.31.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pre-commit>=3.7.1",
    "pytest>=7.4.0",
    "pytest-django>=4.5.0",
    "pytest-cov>=4.1.0",
    "ruff>=0.1.0",
]

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = ["kbc*", "game*", "adminpanel*", "auth*"]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 88
target-version = "py311"
exclude = [
    ".venv",
    "venv",
    "node_modules",
    "migrations",
    "__pycache__",
]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = [
    "E",    # pycodestyle errors
    "W",    # pycodestyle warnings
    "F",    # Pyflakes
    "I",    # isort
    "B",    # flake8-bugbear
    "C4",   # flake8-comprehensions
    "UP",   # pyupgrade
]
ignore = [
    "E501",  # line too long (handled by formatter)
]

[tool.ruff.lint.isort]
known-first-party = ["game", "adminpanel", "auth", "kbc"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE = "kbc.settings"
DJANGO_CONFIGURATION = "Dev"
python_files = ["test_*.py", "*_test.py"]
addopts = "-v --tb=short"
testpaths = ["tests", "game", "adminpanel", "auth"]

[tool.coverage.run]
source = ["."]
omit = [
    "*/migrations/*",
    "*/__pycache__/*",
    ".venv/*",
    "venv/*",
    "manage.py",
    "*/tests/*",
    "conftest.py",
    "tailwind_theme/*",
]

[tool.coverage.report]
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "def __repr__",
    "raise AssertionError",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
]

content.text:
Here's the analysis of your CLAUDE.md:

---

## Issues Found

### 1. PR Template Mismatch (lines 24-32)
The CLAUDE.md shows the template body as:
```
Fixes <linear-ticket-or-issue>
```
But `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` actually uses:
```
Fixes #...
```
These are inconsistent. Claude will follow the CLAUDE.md version, which differs from the real template.

### 2. Branch Naming Example Contains a Ticket ID (line 9)
```
steadyfall/tri-60-test-usernamevalidator-...
```
This includes `tri-60` (a Linear ticket ID), but the PR section says **"Do not include ticket IDs"**. The branch convention doesn't explicitly say whether ticket IDs in branches are allowed or not — the example implies they are. This is ambiguous.

### 3. Test Command Is Too Specific (line 48)
```bash
source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file> -v --tb=short
```
`-v --tb=short` are already in `pyproject.toml` under `addopts`, so they're redundant here. Also, no command is given for running the **full test suite**, which is a common need.

### 4. Linting Tool Not Mentioned
`pyproject.toml` has `ruff` configured (with `pre-commit`), but CLAUDE.md has no mention of it. Claude won't know to run `uv run ruff check .` or `uv run ruff format .` before committing.

### 5. Test Path Scope Is Narrower Than Config (line 57)
CLAUDE.md says: `Test file location: tests/unit/`
But `pyproject.toml` sets `testpaths = ["tests", "game", "adminpanel", "auth"]` — tests also exist alongside app code. This could mislead Claude into only writing tests in `tests/unit/`.

---

## What's Working Well

- Clear conventional commits format with allowed types
- Branch naming convention is explicit with a 60-char limit
- `@pytest.mark.django_db` rule is a good specific guard
- The "read source before writing tests" rule is high-value
- Worktree + `make setup` + `.env` sourcing reminders are all practical

---

## Suggested Fixes

```diff
- **Follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`**:
  ```
- Fixes <linear-ticket-or-issue>
+ Fixes #...
  ```

+ ## Linting
+ Run before committing: `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format .`

- **Test file location**: `tests/unit/`
+ **Test file location**: `tests/unit/` for unit tests; app-level tests go alongside their app (e.g. `game/tests/`)

- source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file> -v --tb=short
+ # Run a specific file:
+ source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file>
+ # Run all tests:
+ source .env && uv run pytest
```

Would you like me to apply these fixes directly to `CLAUDE.md`?

message.content: apply these fixes to CLAUDE.md, except for issue 1 - make it reference the template file directly, issue 4 - comment out. Make it refer to @docs/RUNBOOK.md for more helpful commands as well.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to apply fixes to CLAUDE.md with these specific instructions:
1. Issue 1 (PR template mismatch) - reference the template file directly instead of copying it
2. Issue 2 (branch naming ambiguity) - apply the fix
3. Issue 3 (test command redundancy) - apply the fix
4. Issue 4 (linting) - comment it out (not add it as a full section, but comment it out or add as a commented note)
5. Issue 5 (test path scope) - apply the fix
6. Also add a reference to `docs/RUNBOOK.md` for more helpful commands


Now I'm reviewing the current CLAUDE.md structure to understand what needs to be modified. I can see the repository info, branch naming conventions, and commit message format. I need to go through each issue and apply the corresponding fixes while keeping the document clean and well-organized. I'm looking at the testing setup and conventions — sourcing the environment file before running pytest with the Django configuration, making sure all tests pass without skips, and using the database marker for tests that need it. I should also read the actual source code before writing tests to understand the real behavior rather than making assumptions.

Now I'm reviewing the documentation changes needed: the PR template should just reference the file directly instead of showing the full content inline, and I need to clarify the branch naming convention — specifically that ticket IDs are allowed in branch names but not in PR titles, since the current example with `tri-60` creates ambiguity about what's actually permitted.

For the test command, I should remove the redundant flags that are already configured in pyproject.toml and add a separate command for running the full test suite. As for linting, I'm thinking about how to handle this in markdown — the user wants it commented out, which probably means adding it as an HTML comment or keeping it as a note that's not prominently displayed, since linting uses ruff but shouldn't be a main focus in the documentation.

I also need to update the test file location to cover both places where tests can be run, and add a reference to the RUNBOOK documentation. Now I'm drafting the updated CLAUDE.md file with these changes incorporated. For testing, I need to configure Django settings through environment variables and source the `.env` file before running pytest, whether it's a specific test file or the full suite. Tests touching the database require the `@pytest.mark.django_db` decorator, integration tests use Django's test client, and unit tests for pure logic don't need database access. Test files go in `tests/unit/` for unit tests or alongside their respective app directories, and all tests must pass without skips or failures.

When writing tests, I should read the actual source code first to understand redirect URLs and behavior rather than guessing. I'm also using git worktrees for parallel feature branches to keep work isolated from main, running `make setup` in each new worktree and sourcing `.env` since it doesn't copy automatically. For deployment and operational tasks, I can reference the RUNBOOK.md documentation.

  Fixes <linear-ticket-or-issue>

input.content:
# Trivivo

## Repository
- **Repo**: `steadyfall/wwbm-webapp`
- **Main branch**: `main`

## Git & Commits

**Branch naming**: `<username>/descriptive-name` (e.g. `steadyfall/tri-60-test-usernamevalidator-...`)
- Ticket IDs are allowed in branch names but **not** in PR titles
- Max 60 characters total

**Commit messages**: Conventional Commits format
```
<type>: short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `test`, `build`, `chore`, `style`, `refactor`

## Pull Requests

- **Always create PRs as drafts** (`--draft`) targeting `main`
- **Do not include ticket IDs in PR titles** (no `TRI-xx` or issue numbers)
- **Follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`** for PR body structure
- Keep PR body ≤ 500 words
- Include screenshots/pictures for any frontend/visual changes

<!-- Linting: run `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format .` before committing (configured in pyproject.toml) -->

## Testing

**Framework**: `pytest` with `pytest-django`

**Config**: `pyproject.toml`
```
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=kbc.settings
DJANGO_CONFIGURATION=Dev
```

**Before running tests, source the env file**:
```bash
# Run a specific file:
source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file>

# Run all tests:
source .env && uv run pytest
```

**Rules**:
- All tests must pass — no skips, no failures
- Use `@pytest.mark.django_db` for any test that touches the database
- Integration tests use `django.test.Client`
- Unit tests for validators/pure logic require no DB

**Test file location**: `tests/unit/` for unit tests; app-level tests go alongside their app (e.g. `game/tests/`)

## General Conventions

- **Read source code before writing tests** — never assume redirect URLs, message levels, or behavior; read the actual view/function first
- Use git worktrees when working on parallel feature branches to avoid interfering with `main`
- Run `make setup` when in freshly initialized git worktree
- Source `.env` in every worktree (it is not copied automatically)

## References

- See `docs/RUNBOOK.md` for deployment, database management, and operational commands

## Repository
- **Repo**: `steadyfall/wwbm-webapp`
- **Main branch**: `main`

## Git & Commits

**Branch naming**: `<username>/descriptive-name` (e.g. `steadyfall/tri-60-test-usernamevalidator-...`)
- Ticket IDs are allowed in branch names but **not** in PR titles
- Max 60 characters total

**Commit messages**: Conventional Commits format
```
<type>: short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `test`, `build`, `chore`, `style`, `refactor`

## Pull Requests

- **Always create PRs as drafts** (`--draft`) targeting `main`
- **Do not include ticket IDs in PR titles** (no `TRI-xx` or issue numbers)
- **Follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`** for PR body structure
- Keep PR body ≤ 500 words
- Include screenshots/pictures for any frontend/visual changes

<!-- Linting: run `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format .` before committing (configured in pyproject.toml) -->

## Testing

**Framework**: `pytest` with `pytest-django`

**Config**: `pyproject.toml`
```
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=kbc.settings
DJANGO_CONFIGURATION=Dev
```

**Before running tests, source the env file**:
```bash
# Run a specific file:
source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file>

# Run all tests:
source .env && uv run pytest
```

**Rules**:
- All tests must pass — no skips, no failures
- Use `@pytest.mark.django_db` for any test that touches the database
- Integration tests use `django.test.Client`
- Unit tests for validators/pure logic require no DB

**Test file location**: `tests/unit/` for unit tests; app-level tests go alongside their app (e.g. `game/tests/`)

## General Conventions

- **Read source code before writing tests** — never assume redirect URLs, message levels, or behavior; read the actual view/function first
- Use git worktrees when working on parallel feature branches to avoid interfering with `main`
- Run `make setup` when in freshly initialized git worktree
- Source `.env` in every worktree (it is not copied automatically)

## References

- See `docs/RUNBOOK.md` for deployment, database management, and operational commands

# Trivivo

## Repository
- **Repo**: `steadyfall/wwbm-webapp`
- **Main branch**: `main`

## Git & Commits

**Branch naming**: `<username>/descriptive-name` (e.g. `steadyfall/tri-60-test-usernamevalidator-...`)

Max character limit for branch name: 60 characters total

**Commit messages**: Conventional Commits format
```
<type>: short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `test`, `build`, `chore`, `style`, `refactor`

## Pull Requests

- **Always create PRs as drafts** (`--draft`) targeting `main`
- **Do not include ticket IDs in PR titles** (no `TRI-xx` or issue numbers)
- **Follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`**:
  ```
  Fixes <linear-ticket-or-issue>

  ### Description
  <!-- problem being fixed -->

  ### Tests
  <!-- tests written/done -->
  ```
- Keep PR body ≤ 500 words
- Include screenshots/pictures for any frontend/visual changes

## Testing

**Framework**: `pytest` with `pytest-django`

**Config**: `pyproject.toml`
```
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=kbc.settings
DJANGO_CONFIGURATION=Dev
```

**Before running tests, source the env file**:
```bash
source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file> -v --tb=short
```

**Rules**:
- All tests must pass — no skips, no failures
- Use `@pytest.mark.django_db` for any test that touches the database
- Integration tests use `django.test.Client`
- Unit tests for validators/pure logic require no DB

**Test file location**: `tests/unit/`

## General Conventions

- **Read source code before writing tests** — never assume redirect URLs, message levels, or behavior; read the actual view/function first
- Use git worktrees when working on parallel feature branches to avoid interfering with `main`
- Run `make setup` when in freshly initialized git worktree
- Source `.env` in every worktree (it is not copied automatically)

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Issue 1** (PR template): Replaced the inlined copy with a direct reference to `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`
- **Issue 2** (branch naming): Added a clarifying bullet — ticket IDs are allowed in branch names but not PR titles
- **Issue 3** (test command): Removed redundant `-v --tb=short` flags (already in `pyproject.toml`); added a full-suite command alongside the single-file one
- **Issue 4** (linting): Added as an HTML comment (`<!-- ... -->`) — visible in source but won't render in the UI
- **Issue 5** (test paths): Updated to note that app-level tests live alongside their app (e.g. `game/tests/`)
- **RUNBOOK reference**: Added a `## References` section pointing to `docs/RUNBOOK.md`

file.content:
# ==============================================================================
# Trivivo Makefile
# ==============================================================================
# Django project automation for development, testing, and database management.
#
# Usage:
#   make help       - Show this help message
#   make setup      - Complete one-time setup
#   make run        - Start development server
#
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SHELL := /bin/bash
.DEFAULT_GOAL := help

# Project paths
PROJECT_ROOT := $(shell pwd)
VENV_DIR := $(PROJECT_ROOT)/.venv
TAILWIND_SRC := $(PROJECT_ROOT)/tailwind_theme/static_src
DB_FILE := $(PROJECT_ROOT)/db.sqlite3

# Python/Django commands (using uv)
UV := uv
PYTHON := $(UV) run python
MANAGE := $(UV) run python manage.py

# Migration directories for cleanup
MIGRATION_DIRS := game/migrations adminpanel/migrations auth/migrations

# Colors for output
YELLOW := \033[1;33m
GREEN := \033[1;32m
RED := \033[1;31m
CYAN := \033[1;36m
NC := \033[0m

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Help
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: help
help: ## Show this help message
	@printf "$(CYAN)Trivivo - Django Project Commands$(NC)\n"
	@printf "\n"
	@printf "$(GREEN)Primary Commands:$(NC)\n"
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | \
		awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "  $(YELLOW)%-20s$(NC) %s\n", $$1, $$2}'
	@printf "\n"
	@printf "$(RED)Destructive Commands (use with caution):$(NC)\n"
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?##! .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | \
		awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?##! "}; {printf "  $(YELLOW)%-20s$(NC) %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Primary Development Commands
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: setup run run-server run-tailwind check test test-failed

setup: _check-requirements ## Complete one-time setup: venv, deps, tailwind, migrate
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Setting up Trivivo development environment...$(NC)\n"
	$(UV) venv --seed
	$(UV) pip install -e ".[dev]"
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Installing Tailwind dependencies...$(NC)\n"
	cd $(TAILWIND_SRC) && npm install
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Running database migrations...$(NC)\n"
	$(MAKE) migrate
	@printf "$(GREEN)>>> Setup complete! Run 'make run' to start the server.$(NC)\n"

run: ## Start Django development server with Tailwind
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Starting development server...$(NC)\n"
	@trap 'kill %1 2>/dev/null' EXIT; \
		$(MANAGE) tailwind start & \
		$(MANAGE) runserver

run-server: ## Start Django development server only (no Tailwind)
	$(MANAGE) runserver

run-tailwind: ## Start Tailwind CSS watcher only
	$(MANAGE) tailwind start

check: format lint ## Run all code quality checks (format, lint)
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Running Django system checks...$(NC)\n"
	$(MANAGE) check
	@printf "$(GREEN)>>> All checks passed!$(NC)\n"

test: ## Run pytest test suite with coverage reporting
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Running tests with coverage...$(NC)\n"
	source .env && $(UV) run pytest --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing --cov-report=html

test-failed: ## Re-run only failed tests
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Re-running failed tests...$(NC)\n"
	source .env && $(UV) run pytest --lf

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Code Quality & Formatting
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: format lint

format: ## Auto-format code with ruff
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Formatting code with ruff...$(NC)\n"
	$(UV) run ruff format .

lint: ## Run linting with ruff (auto-fixes issues)
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Linting code with ruff...$(NC)\n"
	$(UV) run ruff check --fix .

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Database Management
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: migrations migrate bootstrap seed shell dbshell

migrations: ## Create new Django migrations
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Creating migrations...$(NC)\n"
	$(MANAGE) makemigrations

migrate: ## Apply pending migrations
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Applying migrations...$(NC)\n"
	$(MANAGE) migrate

bootstrap: ## Initialize database with base data (categories, levels, lifelines)
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Bootstrapping database with base data...$(NC)\n"
	$(MANAGE) createcategories
	$(MANAGE) createlevels
	$(MANAGE) createlifelines
	@printf "$(GREEN)>>> Bootstrap complete!$(NC)\n"

seed: ## Populate database with test data from OpenTriviaDB
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Seeding database with questions...$(NC)\n"
	@FILE_NAME=$$($(MANAGE) fetchqns 10) && \
		printf "$(CYAN)>>> Fetched questions to: $$FILE_NAME$(NC)\n" && \
		$(MANAGE) extractoptions $$FILE_NAME && \
		$(MANAGE) addquestions $$FILE_NAME && \
		rm -f $$FILE_NAME && \
		printf "$(GREEN)>>> Seed complete!$(NC)\n"

shell: ## Open Django shell
	$(MANAGE) shell

dbshell: ## Open database shell
	$(MANAGE) dbshell

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Destructive Commands
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: nuke-db restart _confirm-destructive

_confirm-destructive:
	@printf "$(RED)>>> WARNING: This is a destructive operation!$(NC)\n"
	@read -p "Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] " confirm && \
		[ "$$confirm" = "y" ] || [ "$$confirm" = "Y" ] || \
		(printf "$(YELLOW)>>> Aborted.$(NC)\n" && exit 1)

nuke-db: _confirm-destructive ##! DESTRUCTIVE: Delete database and migration files
	@printf "$(RED)>>> Nuking database and migrations...$(NC)\n"
	rm -f $(DB_FILE)
	@for dir in $(MIGRATION_DIRS); do \
		find $$dir -type f -name '*.py' ! -name '__init__.py' -delete 2>/dev/null || true; \
		printf "$(YELLOW)>>> Cleaned migrations in $$dir$(NC)\n"; \
	done
	@printf "$(GREEN)>>> Database and migrations deleted. Run 'make migrate' to recreate.$(NC)\n"

restart: _confirm-destructive ##! DESTRUCTIVE: Full restart (nuke db, migrate, bootstrap)
	@printf "$(RED)>>> Performing full restart...$(NC)\n"
	rm -f $(DB_FILE)
	@for dir in $(MIGRATION_DIRS); do \
		find $$dir -type f -name '*.py' ! -name '__init__.py' -delete 2>/dev/null || true; \
	done
	$(MAKE) migrate
	$(MAKE) bootstrap
	@printf "$(GREEN)>>> Restart complete!$(NC)\n"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Testing Variants
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: test-functional test-pipeline

test-functional: ## Run functional tests only
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Running functional tests...$(NC)\n"
	source .env && $(UV) run pytest tests/functional/ -v

test-pipeline: ## Run tests with 100% coverage requirement (for CI)
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Running pipeline tests with strict coverage...$(NC)\n"
	source .env && $(UV) run pytest --cov=. --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=100

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Utility Commands
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
.PHONY: clean superuser collectstatic _check-requirements

clean: ## Clean up temporary files and caches
	@printf "$(CYAN)>>> Cleaning up...$(NC)\n"
	find . -type d -name "__pycache__" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type f -name "*.pyc" -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	find . -type f -name ".coverage" -delete 2>/dev/null || true
	rm -rf .pytest_cache htmlcov .mypy_cache .ruff_cache
	@printf "$(GREEN)>>> Cleanup complete!$(NC)\n"

superuser: ## Create a Django superuser
	$(MANAGE) createsuperuser

collectstatic: ## Collect static files for production
	$(MANAGE) collectstatic --noinput

_check-requirements:
	@command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
		(printf "$(RED)Error: 'uv' is not installed. Install it with: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh$(NC)\n" && exit 1)
	@command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
		(printf "$(RED)Error: 'npm' is not installed. Please install Node.js.$(NC)\n" && exit 1)

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The Makefile has a `help` target (`make help`) that shows all commands. The last line of CLAUDE.md says:

```
- Use commands defined in `Makefile` whenever possible for ease of use
```

This is vague. Since `make help` exists and auto-generates a list of all commands, I can optimize the last line to tell Claude to run `make help` to see all available commands, rather than just saying "use commands defined in Makefile". This is more actionable and useful.

I could also update it to:
```
- Run `make help` to see all available commands; prefer `make` targets over raw `uv run python manage.py ...` invocations


```

This is more specific and actionable for Claude, as it knows exactly how to discover available commands. Let me update just that last line.

I'm also noticing the test setup—the Makefile's `test` target runs with coverage and sources the environment, while CLAUDE.md mentions a raw pytest command. Since `make test` already handles the environment setup and coverage configuration, I should verify whether the documentation needs updating to point toward the Makefile target instead.

For the last line specifically, I want to be more prescriptive. Instead of just saying to use Makefile commands, I should tell the user how to discover them and why it's better than raw invocations.

```

content.text: The Makefile has a `make help` target that auto-generates a full command listing. The last line is vague — it can be made more actionable by pointing directly to `make help` and being prescriptive about preferring `make` over raw `uv` invocations.

toolUseResult.oldString: - Use commands defined in `Makefile` whenever possible for ease of use
toolUseResult.newString: - Run `make help` to list all available commands; prefer `make` targets over raw `uv run python manage.py ...` invocations
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Trivivo

## Repository
- **Repo**: `steadyfall/wwbm-webapp`
- **Main branch**: `main`

## Git & Commits

**Branch naming**: `<username>/descriptive-name` (e.g. `steadyfall/tri-60-test-usernamevalidator-...`)
- Ticket IDs are allowed in branch names but **not** in PR titles
- Max 60 characters total

**Commit messages**: Conventional Commits format
```
<type>: short description
```
Types: `feat`, `fix`, `test`, `build`, `chore`, `style`, `refactor`

## Pull Requests

- **Always create PRs as drafts** (`--draft`) targeting `main`
- **Do not include ticket IDs in PR titles** (no `TRI-xx` or issue numbers)
- **Follow `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`** for PR body structure
- Keep PR body ≤ 500 words
- Include screenshots/pictures for any frontend/visual changes

<!-- Linting: run `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format .` before committing (configured in pyproject.toml) -->

## Testing

**Framework**: `pytest` with `pytest-django`

**Config**: `pyproject.toml`
```
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=kbc.settings
DJANGO_CONFIGURATION=Dev
```

**Before running tests, source the env file**:
```bash
# Run a specific file:
source .env && uv run pytest tests/unit/<file>

# Run all tests:
source .env && uv run pytest
```

**Rules**:
- All tests must pass — no skips, no failures
- Use `@pytest.mark.django_db` for any test that touches the database
- Integration tests use `django.test.Client`
- Unit tests for validators/pure logic require no DB

**Test file location**: `tests/unit/` for unit tests; app-level tests go alongside their app (e.g. `game/tests/`)

## General Conventions

- **Read source code before writing tests** — never assume redirect URLs, message levels, or behavior; read the actual view/function first
- Use git worktrees when working on parallel feature branches to avoid interfering with `main`
- Run `make setup` when in freshly initialized git worktree
- Source `.env` in every worktree (it is not copied automatically)

## References

- See `docs/RUNBOOK.md` for deployment, database management, and operational commands
- Use commands defined in `Makefile` whenever possible for ease of use

lines: - See `docs/RUNBOOK.md` for deployment, database management, and operational commands
-- Use commands defined in `Makefile` whenever possible for ease of use
+- Run `make help` to list all available commands; prefer `make` targets over raw `uv run python manage.py ...` invocations

content.text:
The old line was a passive suggestion ("whenever possible"). The new line is actionable:

- **`make help`** — tells Claude exactly how to discover commands rather than having to read the Makefile
- **"prefer `make` targets over raw `uv run python manage.py ...`"** — explicit and prescriptive, matching the pattern the Makefile itself enforces
